perturbation.original_text: 1.1 PPG SHANGHAI does hereby appoint DISTRIBUTOR to distribute in the Territory the Products (as defined in paragraph 2, below) upon the terms and conditions hereinafter set forth. DISTRIBUTOR does hereby accept such appointment.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 PPG SHANGHAI does hereby appoint NeoMedia Micro Paint Repair to distribute in the Territory the Products (as defined in paragraph 2, below) upon the terms and conditions hereinafter set forth. NeoMedia Micro Paint Repair does hereby accept such appointment.
perturbation.explanation: This change replaces 'DISTRIBUTOR' with 'NeoMedia Micro Paint Repair' in Section 1.1. This is problematic because 'Distributor' is a defined term referring to the entity entering the agreement. However, Section B of the preamble indicates NeoMedia Micro Paint Repair China, a Wholley Foreign Owned Enterprise ('WFOE'), will eventually assume the 'Distributor' title. Therefore, substituting the parent company (NeoMedia Micro Paint Repair) for 'Distributor' creates an uncertainty regarding which entity is initially bound by the agreement and when the transfer to the WFOE should occur.
perturbation.contradicted_text: B. DISTRIBUTOR has the means to market Products in the Territory. As soon as Distributor's China subsidary, NeoMeida Micro Paint Repair China, a Wholley Foreign Owned Enterprise ("WFOE") registered under the Chinese laws and regulations, will be established, this title of Distributor will be automatically switched to the WFOE.
perturbation.justification: The modified text replaces 'DISTRIBUTOR' with 'NeoMedia Micro Paint Repair'. The contradicted text, Section B, states that the 'Distributor' title will automatically switch to 'NeoMedia Micro Paint Repair China, a Wholley Foreign Owned Enterprise ("WFOE")' upon its establishment. By directly appointing 'NeoMedia Micro Paint Repair' (the parent company) as the initial distributor instead of the undefined 'Distributor', the modified text creates ambiguity and contradiction about which entity is initially bound by the agreement and when the transfer to the WFOE should occur, violating the Misaligned Terminology definition and creating uncertainty. This directly conflicts with the intended transfer mechanism outlined in Section B.

perturbation.original_text:
4. DISTRIBUTOR'S RESPONSIBILITY
4.1 DISTRIBUTOR shall promote the sale of the Products in the Territory. DISTRIBUTOR will conduct its operations in the Territory through offices or agencies to be maintained by DISTRIBUTOR at its sole cost and expense.
perturbation.changed_text:
4. RESPONSIBLE PARTY'S RESPONSIBILITY
4.1 RESPONSIBLE PARTY shall promote the sale of the Products in the Territory. RESPONSIBLE PARTY will conduct its operations in the Territory through offices or agencies to be maintained by RESPONSIBLE PARTY at its sole cost and expense.
perturbation.explanation: This change replaces 'DISTRIBUTOR' with 'RESPONSIBLE PARTY' in Section 4.1. The agreement consistently uses 'DISTRIBUTOR' to define the entity responsible for marketing and selling the products. Replacing this term introduces ambiguity as to who holds this responsibility. This becomes particularly confusing in other sections where 'DISTRIBUTOR' is specifically referenced with the expectation of this marketing and sales role.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. DISTRIBUTOR'S RESPONSIBILITIES DISTRIBUTOR agrees that DISTRIBUTOR shall, use all reasonable efforts , do the following: (a) provide its customers with services regarding the Products, including the safety and toxicological aspects of Products handling and ensure that delivery to customers is made of technical information provided by PPG SHANGHAI regarding Products, including the safety precautions and toxicological aspects of Products handling; (b) maintain an adequate supply of Products to expedite customer deliveries and give prompt and efficient service to its customers in the Territory; (c) maintain knowledge of the market in the Territory and regularly communicate such knowledge to PPG SHANGHAI; (d) be responsible that the labels for the Products meet all governmental and all applicable laws of the Territory regulatory requirements and comply with all applicable laws of the Territory; (e) provide to its customers, Product technical support and training and ensure its customers maintain technical support and training to end-users. (f) plan and develop promotional and advertising strategies to enhance PPG SHANGHAI's image and sales value in the region. (g) Achieve agreed and signed sales target (Appendix 3).
perturbation.justification: The original agreement uses 'DISTRIBUTOR' consistently to define the entity responsible for marketing and sales. The change replaces 'DISTRIBUTOR' with 'RESPONSIBLE PARTY' in Section 4.1. This replacement introduces ambiguity and contradicts Section 9, which explicitly states 'DISTRIBUTOR'S RESPONSIBILITIES,' creating confusion about who is responsible for the duties outlined therein. Therefore, the modified text introduces a strong contradiction because the duties of the distributor are now unclear due to the change in terminology.

perturbation.original_text: 11.1 PPG SHANGHAI warrants only its title to the Products and that the &bbsp; Products will be as set forth in the warranty statement, if any, on the Products' labeling or in the absence of any such warranty statement that the Products will conform to PPG SHANGHAI's standard warranty when they are taken from PPG SHANGHAI's warehouse by DISTRIUTOR or its transport agent.
perturbation.changed_text: 11.1 PPG SHANGHAI warrants only its title to the Products and that the &bbsp; Products will be as set forth in the warranty statement, if any, on the Products' labeling or in the absence of any such warranty statement that the Products will conform to PPG SHANGHAI's standard warranty when they are taken from PPG SHANGHAI's warehouse by the party picking them up or its transport agent.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'DISTRIBUTOR' with 'the party picking them up' in Section 11.1 creates ambiguity regarding responsibility for the products once they leave PPG SHANGHAI's warehouse. The original text clearly assigns this responsibility to the distributor or their agent. The modified text introduces uncertainty as to whether anyone else could be responsible for picking up and handling the products, such as a customer directly purchasing from PPG SHANGHAI, conflicting with the established distributor model. This is contradictory in section 11.3 where there is reference to distributor
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.3 DISTRIBUTOR assumes all responsibility, risk and liability arising from (i) the unloading, discharge, storage, handling and use of the Products, including use thereof alone or in combination with other substances; (ii) the improper functioning or failure of unloading, discharge, transportation or storage systems equipment used by DISTRIBUTOR, whether furnished or recommended by PPG SHANGHAI or not; and (iii) the failure to comply with laws, rules and regulations governing unloading, discharge, storage, handling and use of the Products.
perturbation.justification: The original text in 11.1 specifically assigns responsibility for the products to the 'DISTRIBUTOR' once they leave PPG SHANGHAI's warehouse. However, the changed text replaces 'DISTRIBUTOR' with 'the party picking them up,' creating ambiguity and potentially shifting responsibility to someone other than the distributor. This directly contradicts section 11.3, which states that 'DISTRIBUTOR assumes all responsibility, risk and liability.' The changed text undermines the clarity and enforceability of the distributor's responsibility as defined in 11.3, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
